concept:
Tested 3 2006/03/09 22:55 Renal tubular acidosis (RTA) associated with abnormal H+ secretion and nephrolithiasis is called: Type I (distal) RTA
Renal tubular acidosis (RTA) associated with abnormal HCO3- and rickets is called: Type II (proximal) RTA
Renal tubular acidosis (RTA) associated with aldosterone defect is called: Type IV (distal) RTA
"Doughy skin" is caused by: Hypernatremia
Differential of hypervolemic hyponatremia is:
Cirrhosis
CHF
Nephritic syndrome
Chvostek's and Trousseau's signs are: Hypocalcemia
Most common causes of hypercalcemia are:
Malignancy
Hyperparathyroidism
T-wave flattening and U waves are caused by: Hypokalemia
Peaked T waves and widened QRS is caused by: Hyperkalemia
First line treatment for moderate hypercalcemia is: IV hydration and loop diuretics (furosemide)
Type of Acute Renal Failure (ARF) in patient with FE(Na) less than 1% is: Prerenal
Patient:
is 49 year old
is male
with acute-onset flank pain
with hematuria Diagnosis? Nephrolithiasis
Most common type of nephrolithiasis is: Calcium oxalate
Patient:
is 20 years old
is male
with a palpable flank mass
with hematuria
with Ultrasound (U/S) shows bilateral enlarged kidneys and cysts Associated brain anomaly is: Cerebral berry aneurysms (AD PcKD)
Hematuria
Hypertension (HTN)
Oliguria is: Nephritic Syndrome
Proteinuria
Hypoalbuminemia
Hyperlipidemia
Hyperlipiduria
Edema is: Nephrotic syndrome
Most common form of nephritic syndrome is: Membranous glomerulonephritis
Most common form of glomerulonephritis is: IgA nephropathy (Berger's disease)
Glomerulonephritis with deafness is: Alport's syndrome
Glomerulonephritis with hemoptsys is:
Wegener's granulomatosis
Goodpasture's syndrome
Presence of red cell casts in urine sediment indicate: Glomerulonephritis/nephritic syndrome
Eosinophils in urine sediment indicate: Allergic interstitial nephritis
Waxy casts in urine sediment and Maltese crosses (seen with lipiduria) are caused by: Nephrotic syndrome
Drowsiness
Asterixis
Nausea
Pericardial friction rub is: Uremic syndrome seen in patients with renal failure
A 55 year-old man is diagnosed with prostate cancer. Treatment options?
Wait
Surgical resection
Radiation
Androgen suppression
Low urine specific gravity in the presence of high serum osmolality is caused by: DI
Treatment of SIADH is:
Fluid restriction
Demeclocycline
Hematuria
Flank pain
Palpable flank mass are probably caused by: Renal cell carcinoma (RCC)
Testicular cancer associated with B-hCG, AFP is: Choriocarcinoma
Complication of overly rapid correction of hyponatremia is: Central pontine myelinosis
Salicylate ingestion results in what type of acid-base disorder? Anion gap acidosis and primary respiratory alkalosis due to central respiratory stimulation
Acid-base disturbance commonly seen in pregnant women is: Respiratory alkalosis
Three systemic diseases causing nephrotic syndrome are:
DM
SLE
Amyloidosis
Elevated erythropoietin level, elevated HCT and normal O2 saturation suggest what? What is the work-up?
Suspect: RCC or other erythropoietin-producing tumor
Work-up: Evaluate with CT scan
A 55 year-old presents with irritative and obstructive urinary symptoms. Diagnosis? Treatment options?
Suspect: BPH
Treatment options:
No treatment
Terazosin
Finasteride
Surgical intervention (TURP)
